examples: The phrase "substance abuse" is often used instead of "drug abuse" to make clear that substances such as alcohol and tobacco can be just as harmfully misused as heroin and cocaine.
Frequent excessive consumption can harmfully interfere with the user's well-being.
Many new drugs are aimed at old people who may already be taking several other pills. Testers have to be sure that they will not interact harmfully.
More than a million people in Scotland are drinking hazardously or harmfully.
The theory advanced by global warming alarmists is that carbon dioxide emissions, the alleged instigator of the modest rise in 20th century temperatures, cause oceans to become harmfully acidic.
